title: Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: "Instrumentality"             Quote Post by arkiel » Mon Nov 21, 2011 12:31 am So, in the course of my shitty law school studies, specifically my Torts course, we did a number of cases involving the uncontested manipulation of one party upon another (usually in the absence of that other party's agency -- i.e., an anesthetized patient being acted upon by doctors). That uncontested manipulation is referred to as "instrumentality." Naturally, this got me thinking, and since I haven't done a terribly embarrassing college-level Independent Studies project impressing subtle Christian mechanics onto the plot of Eva, I figured the world yet owes me a chance to flap off and make an ass of myself. I was wondering if anyone had explored the Instrumentality of Man Project as the sequence of events beginning with the discovery of the White Giant, and proceeding on through Second Impact and the 'Angel War', as a way of channeling enough planetary resources into one focal point (Tokyo-3, NERV, Eva-tech) to survive and set the stage for 3I. The Instrumentality in question was Seele, the Other was humanity. According to the wiki, Instrumentality and Complementation were just Anno fucking around, using both terms to mean the same thing. Depressing. If only as a way to conceptualize some of the trickier parts of the plot, it would make so much more sense to consider Instrumentality as being White Giant to 3I, with the goal of setting the stage from Complementation -- everyone being tanged and made 'complete.' But the community has already pretty much adopted Instrumentality as meaning 'to be made complete,' so. Yeah. This is just me talking.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Nov 21, 2011 12:51 am I always wonder why Anno (or some of his staff) decided to use Instrumentality as the English word for the Japanese 補完 in 人類補完計画 rather than Complementation.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Nov 21, 2011 1:01 am symbv wrote: I always wonder why Anno (or some of his staff) decided to use Instrumentality as the English word for the Japanese 補完 in 人類補完計画 rather than Complementation.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Nov 21, 2011 1:20 am ^ I see. That makes sense now. In Japan, his Instrumentality of Mankind series was translated as 人類補完機構, which literally means "Human Complementation Organisation". I guess Anno just did a flipover and back-"translated" 補完 (which as Complementation fits into what it is about in Eva) into Instrumentality. I am not sure if he really knew the exact meaning of the word Instrumentality but I guess he would not care even if he knew.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Nov 21, 2011 1:59 am The chamber music motif in Death seems to suggest that he was aware.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Nov 21, 2011 2:04 am In what way the chamber music in Death is linked to Instrumentality's meaning in English language?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 21, 2011 7:04 am It still works as written. Instrumentality means to work through something else as a means or tool, and humanity itself is used in this manner to accomplish their goals both before (as the workforce to create the necessary means) and during (as the raw materials) 3rd impact.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Mon Nov 21, 2011 12:31 pm NemZ wrote: It still works as written. Instrumentality means to work through something else as a means or tool, and humanity itself is used in this manner to accomplish their goals both before (as the workforce to create the necessary means) and during (as the raw materials) 3rd impact.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 21, 2011 4:01 pm arkiel wrote: Instrumentality ceased, a human heart, wild and untamed, decided to love itself despite being ugly and imperfect, and Complementation for a whole race failed as collateral.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Mon Nov 21, 2011 4:06 pm Is that the state of the art now? That 25-26 and 25'-26' aren't parallel occurrences? Complementation still collapsed as consequence of Shinji accepting/rejecting/acting on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Nov 21, 2011 4:20 pm arkiel wrote: Is that the state of the art now?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 21, 2011 4:27 pm arkiel wrote: Is that the state of the art now? That 25-26 and 25'-26' aren't parallel occurrences?

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Mon Nov 21, 2011 5:19 pm But whose Instrumentality resulted in an ocean of LCL and two people on a beach? Was that was Gendo wanted? Rei? SEELE? SEELE wanted to be an Angel, or become first among equals in a hive-mind, or something. That didn't happen. Their Instrumentality was interfered with. Gendo wanted Yui. He got Yui. But his investment in Complementation ended when Unit One bit his head off. He was probably indifferent to any conclusion aside from Yui. Or, we're given no fucking evidence that he had any aim aside from seeing his wife again. Could totally be wrong about this. Would like to be. Maybe it was all down to Rei. Earlier I said she put Shinji in charge because of 'love', (which disconnected any Instrumentality of her's from what followed) but maybe her desire for death was such that she put Shinji in charge as a goal-oriented action, knowing he would reject Complementation, and that that rejection would result in her death. ^that's an argument for (a form of) Instrumentality driving Complementation further into the process than may be casually inferred. But the hopeful outcome on the beach was, as near I can see, no one's goal. Assuming Shinji automatism is state of the art. If you want to go another way with it, you probably could. (FYI, I'm using state of the art here as the meaning the community is currently settled on. Recentness is irrelevant. Can you tell I'm reading more case-law than narrative fiction?)

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Mon Nov 21, 2011 7:43 pm arkiel wrote: But whose Instrumentality resulted in an ocean of LCL and two people on a beach? Was that was Gendo wanted? Rei? SEELE?

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Nov 22, 2011 3:24 pm symbv wrote: In what way the chamber music in Death is linked to Instrumentality's meaning in English language?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Nov 22, 2011 7:16 pm ^ Hmm... I hope there are indeed some puns in there, though I cannot help the feeling that it may be a pun noticed more by English speakers than intended.....

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Nov 22, 2011 11:48 pm Maybe "pun" is the wrong word, but I see it as the reason Anno went out of his way to give the other children new musical abilities. Those Death scenes take place in the same setting as TV 25/26, so I think he's trying to tie the idea of musical synchronicity to instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Nov 23, 2011 12:15 am arkiel wrote: But whose Instrumentality resulted in an ocean of LCL and two people on a beach?

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Nov 23, 2011 6:07 pm Well, there's something else. Shinji at the heart of things probably had free will. Or the potential for it. The events of the series weaponized him though, tempered him to the point where the whole experience was inevitable -- which is probably the best possible outcome for him, since a human with true free will would probably have his ego dissolve like sugar in coffee. If you take Shinj's 'weaponization' as an act of instrumentality by those that acted upon him, we've got a near-complete chart of the different instrumentalities that contributed to 3I and Complementation. I'm not sure I get your reading of Rei. Specifically Rei3 having more will to live than Rei2. Even assuming she was using death to mean becoming one with Lilith (and Shiniji later, bow chika wow wow), all I got from Lilith in EoE was a certain amount of Rei-lief, which my mind has since twisted into the giddiness of an immortal creature billions of years old, purpose fulfilled, finally being allowed to dive into a blessed, ending dark.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Nov 23, 2011 6:33 pm arkiel wrote: Well, there's something else. Shinji at the heart of things probably had free will. Or the potential for it.
